title: Hexaphoma ferruginea
intro: Hexaphoma ferruginea is a plant species from the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the Ironwood plant. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hexaphoma ferruginea include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hexaphoma ferruginea is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to harness its health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine practices.
